title: The Sunday Wind-Up Ritual
subtitle: A short ritual for closing the week and setting a single intention for the one ahead
excerpt: A ten-minute Sunday evening ritual for closing the working week and entering the next one deliberately. Reviews what was done, releases what is unfinished or incomplete, and sets one intention for the week ahead.
sourceNotes: Written for homemade.education. The five-part ritual structure draws on the ceremony shape from MONEY Journal (Rebecca Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use on Sunday evening to close the week and enter the next one with one clear intention rather than a list of everything that didn't get done.

content.text: A ten-minute Sunday evening ritual for closing the week deliberately. The practice reviews what was done, releases what is incomplete, and sets one intention for the week ahead, rather than carrying the whole undone list into Monday.

On Sunday evening, take five minutes with a notebook before the week is fully done. Write down three things you did this week that moved something forward. Not the full list. Three. Then write the one thing you most want to move forward in the week ahead.
I am ready to release what didn't get done this week without carrying it as a failure into the next. I release it now. I release it now. I release it now.
I am ready to allow next week to begin with one clear intention rather than last week's leftovers. I allow it now. I allow it now. I allow it now.
Read the three things you did this week. Sit with them for one minute. The week was not empty even if it didn't feel like enough.
Next week has one intention. I begin from there.
Say it once. Close the notebook. The week is done.
Written for homemade.education. The five-part ritual structure draws on the ceremony shape from MONEY Journal (Rebecca Page, 2025).
